title: API - Attempt to create validation record without 'Reason (why and intent)'
preconditions:
1. API endpoint for creating validation records is accessible.
2. Authentication token is available.
steps: Construct an API request payload to create a new validation record.
Ensure the 'Reason (why and intent)' field is either omitted or sent with an empty/null value in the payload.
Send the POST request to the validation record creation API endpoint.
expected_result:
The API response should have a status code indicating a client error (e.g., 400 Bad Request).
The response body should contain an error message explicitly stating that 'Reason (why and intent)' is a mandatory field.

title: UI - Attempt to create validation record without 'Reason (why and intent)'
preconditions:
1. User is logged into the application with appropriate permissions.
2. User is on the page to create a new validation record.
steps: Fill in all other mandatory fields for creating a validation record.
Leave the 'Reason (why and intent)' field empty.
expected_result:
The application should prevent the creation of the record.
A mandatory error message, such as 'Reason (why and intent) field is required', should be displayed next to the 'Reason (why and intent)' field or as a general form error.
The user should remain on the creation page.

title: API - Update correction record by only removing an existing parameter
preconditions:
1. API endpoint for creating and updating correction records is accessible.
2. Authentication token is available.
3. A correction record exists with at least one parameter (e.g., AXLE-CONFIG = 2X0).
steps: Retrieve the existing correction record details via API.
Construct an API request payload for updating the correction record.
In the update payload, remove the previously existing parameter (e.g., AXLE-CONFIG = 2X0) without adding any new parameters or features, or modifying any other existing ones.
Send the PUT/PATCH request to the correction record update API endpoint.
expected_result:
The API response should have a status code indicating a client error (e.g., 400 Bad Request).
The response body should contain an error message: 'At least one Parameter or Feature should be either Added or Removed'.
The correction record should not be updated in the system.

title: UI - Update correction record by only removing an existing parameter
preconditions:
1. User is logged into the application with appropriate permissions.
2. A correction record exists with at least one parameter (e.g., AXLE-CONFIG = 2X0).
3. User is on the page to edit the existing correction record.
steps: Locate the existing parameter (e.g., AXLE-CONFIG = 2X0) in the correction record.
Remove this parameter from the record (e.g., by clicking a delete icon next to it).
Ensure no other parameters or features are added or modified.
expected_result:
The application should prevent the update of the record.
An error message, such as 'At least one Parameter or Feature should be either Added or Removed', should be displayed on the page.
The user should remain on the edit page, and the parameter should still be present or the update should be reverted.
